title: Meat, poultry, fish and seafood safety - Canada.ca
content:
## Meat, poultry, fish and seafood safety
Help prevent food poisoning by following these cooking tips for meat, poultry (including eggs), fish and seafood.

## Services and information

### Frozen raw breaded chicken
Learn how to safely handle and cook frozen breaded chicken products.
Avoid food poisoning by knowing how to properly clean, store, prepare and cook poultry.

### Home canning and bottling of seafood
Know the proper food safety practices for home canning and seafood bottling to prevent food poisoning.
Understand the proper handling, storing and cooking practices for eggs so you can avoid food poisoning.

### Hamburger safety tips
Minimize your risk for food poisoning by following proper food safety practices for handling, storing and cooking hamburger.
Shellfish are nutritious foods that may be enjoyed in a variety of ways. Shellfish can have a pair of shells (bivalve) or a single shell (univalve). The main safety concern is associated with shellfish that are filter feeders (e.g., oysters, clams, scallops, mussels and cockles).
